model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google DeepMind • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Illinois enters the tournament as one of the nation's most potent offensive teams, ranking first in adjusted offensive efficiency according to KenPom. Led by Big Ten Freshman of the Year Keaton Wagler and a deep supporting cast, the Illini possess a level of shot-making and athleticism that should overwhelm a Penn squad ranked outside the top 150. While the Quakers boast a dangerous shooting trio in TJ Power and Ethan Roberts, they lack the defensive interior presence to consistently stop Illinois' high-powered attack. Expect the Illini to control the tempo and use their superior talent to secure a comfortable victory in this 3/14 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois vs. Penn Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois' elite offensive efficiency and superior talent level make them heavy favorites against a Penn team that lacks the defensive interior presence to contain them.
evidence: Illinois ranks 7th in KenPom and 6th in Torvik, boasting the nation's top adjusted offensive efficiency.
Penn ranks 159th in KenPom and 153rd in Torvik.
Illinois features Big Ten Freshman of the Year Keaton Wagler and former five-star recruit Andrej Stojakovic.
Penn's offense ranks 215th in adjusted efficiency, making it unlikely they can keep pace with Illinois.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
